mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.06% suggests deep easing regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 50,000 net new jobs, signaling labor market weakness that precedes equity selloffs as investors anticipate recession risk and earnings downgrades over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50,000 for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 144,894 thousand requires momentum tracking
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS momentum and forward SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously signals demand destruction across consumer and construction sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand units simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,569 thousand is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: demand-led recession or sharp cyclical downturn
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 99.0 index points for two consecutive months, indicating supply-side contraction and potential stagflation risk that depresses equity valuations over the next 5 trading days as investors reduce cyclical exposure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 99.0 for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 99.8562 is near trigger level
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates, signaling a dual labor market deterioration that triggers equity selloffs over 60 trading days as recession probability increases and earnings forecasts are cut.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 75,000 in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.8% is below trigger but tracking required
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase of economic cycle
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.20) in a predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 270 index points while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2.0%, creating a negative real rate environment that initially supports equity valuations through 20 trading days but signals policy error risk.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above 270 AND FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 268.551 and FEDFUNDS at 0.06 suggest negative real rates
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized growth rate, indicating sub-trend economic expansion that constrains corporate earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors downgrade growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% for two consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 19,088.064 billion requires growth rate calculation
regime_hint: sub-trend growth or secular stagnation regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of sub-trend growth periods, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is not significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 101.0 index points while unemployment (UNRATE) simultaneously falls below 5.0%, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and tight labor markets that support corporate profit margins and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 101.0 AND UNRATE falls below 5.0% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 99.8562 and UNRATE at 5.8% are near trigger thresholds
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight supply and labor conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.18) in a logistic model.
